name: Credit Sage
description_short: Credit Sage is a Delaware-based credit repair firm founded in 2022, disputing inaccurate negative items across all three major credit bureaus via phone-based consultations.
description_long:
Credit Sage LLC is a credit repair company incorporated in Delaware in February 2022, with offices in Claymont, DE, led by CEO Marko Petkovic. Despite being a relatively young entrant to the industry, the company has grown rapidly, amassing over 8,800 reviews on Trustpilot — a remarkable volume for a firm only a few years old. Credit Sage focuses on dispute-based credit repair, working with all three major bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — to challenge negative items that are inaccurate, outdated, or unverifiable on consumer credit reports.

Credit Sage's process begins with a free phone consultation in which a representative assesses the client's credit profile and outlines a dispute strategy. The company handles a broad range of negative item types: collections, charge-offs, late payments, bankruptcies, balance errors, and personal information discrepancies such as incorrect names or addresses. Once enrolled, clients can monitor the progress of their cases through a dedicated online portal at customers.creditsage.com. Pricing is not published on the company's website — fees are quoted during the phone consultation — which has been a recurring source of consumer complaints about unexpected recurring monthly charges disclosed only after signup.

Despite its youth, Credit Sage has built an unusual online presence, with Trustpilot reviews running at a 4.0-star composite. Positive reviewers consistently cite helpful and professional representatives, and some clients report negative item removals within the first 30 days of enrollment. The company's service scope is notably narrower than many established competitors: Credit Sage appears focused primarily on bureau dispute letter campaigns, without offering bundled services such as credit monitoring, identity theft protection, or goodwill letter programs. There is no evidence of a mobile app or a public affiliate program, which differentiates it from more established industry players.

Consumers considering Credit Sage should proceed with careful due diligence. The company holds a BBB rating of B and is not BBB-accredited, with 276 complaints filed in roughly two years of operation — a high complaint velocity relative to its size and age. The most common complaints involve undisclosed recurring monthly charges, difficulty canceling service, and limited credit score improvement after months of fees paid. The absence of publicly disclosed pricing runs contrary to industry best practices and is worth flagging: under the Credit Repair Organizations Act, companies must provide written contracts detailing all fees before services begin. Prospective clients should demand a written contract, review cancellation terms carefully, and verify the total cost of enrollment before committing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Credit Sage's website states they cannot and will not make promises or guarantees as to outcomes. Some customers have obtained refunds through BBB complaint escalation, but no published refund policy exists. Setup fee estimated at $149–$278 based on customer reports; monthly fee estimated at $99 based on customer reports — pricing is not publicly disclosed.

tiers.name: Standard Plan
features: Disputes with all three major credit bureaus
Balance error and personal information corrections

pros: Disputes negative items across all three major credit bureaus simultaneously
Free initial phone consultation with no obligation to enroll
Online client portal allows real-time case tracking
Some clients report negative item removals within the first 30 days
Over 8,800 Trustpilot reviews with a 4.0-star composite rating
cons: Pricing not disclosed publicly — fees quoted only during phone consultation, with customer reports citing $149–$278 setup and ~$99/month
Multiple BBB and Trustpilot complaints cite unexpected recurring charges and difficulty canceling service
BBB rating of B with 276 complaints in approximately two years of operation; not BBB-accredited
No money-back guarantee or published refund policy
Service scope limited to bureau disputes — no credit monitoring, identity theft protection, or goodwill letters
best_for: Consumers with clear inaccurate or unverifiable items on all three credit bureau reports
People who prefer phone-based guidance and case management over DIY dispute tools
Those with multiple collections, charge-offs, or late payments they believe are erroneous
Individuals who need help correcting personal information errors across all three bureaus
diagnosis: Best suited for consumers with documented inaccuracies on their credit reports who want professional dispute assistance. Key caution: pricing is not transparent, complaint volume is high relative to company age, and no money-back guarantee exists — always obtain and review a written contract before enrolling.
services: Credit report analysis across all three major bureaus
Bureau dispute letters for inaccurate or unverifiable negative items
Personal information and address error disputes
Client portal access for case progress tracking
typical_results_timeline: Some clients report initial item removals within 30–60 days; however, meaningful credit score improvement typically requires 3–6 months of consistent dispute activity. Results vary significantly based on the number, type, and verifiability of negative items and cannot be guaranteed.
